steps.title: Factor out y^4
steps.content:
Rewrite each term by factoring out the greatest common factor $$y^4$$: $$72y^5 - 48y^4 = y^4(72y - 48)$$
Set this equal to the given form: $$y^4(72y - 48) = r y^4 (12y - 8)$$. Since $$y^4$$ is not zero for all $$y$$, cancel it: $$72y - 48 = r(12y - 8)$$.

steps.title: Match coefficients to find r
steps.content:
For the equality $$72y - 48 = r(12y - 8)$$ to hold for every $$y$$, equate coefficients of $$y$$: $$12r = 72$$ → $$r = 6$$.
Equate constant terms: $$-8r = -48$$ → $$r = 6$$.
Both give the same value, so $$r = 6$$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
